title: desmodium velutinum subsp. longibracteatum
intro: Desmodium velutinum subsp. longibracteatum is a sub-species of the Desmodium velutinum plant, commonly known as velvet leaf or tick clover, which is a type of legume native to tropical and subtropical regions. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Desmodium velutinum subsp. longibracteatum include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, headache, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Desmodium velutinum subsp. longibracteatum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, capsules, and creams for topical application.
